platform_note: Detailed prototype profile for GHAEX — Ghana Raw Export Index Fund. Prepared for WASI Intelligence platform upload.

fund_family.shared_family_rule: Each country fund keeps its own export methodology while staying aligned to a continent-wide naming, governance, and packaging structure.
fund_family.comparison_currency_note: Publish the country in GHS and publish a continent-wide comparison series in USD.

investment_objective.primary_objective: Track the evolution of the main raw materials exported from Ghana through a transparent, rules-based benchmark measured in GHS.
investment_objective.secondary_objective: Provide a future foundation for a regulated investment product linked to Ghana's export economy and the broader AFEX continental architecture.
economic_thesis: Ghana is the world's second-largest cocoa producer and a major gold exporter, creating a diversified commodity export base.
The Takoradi-Tema port corridor anchors a significant volume of mineral and agricultural export flows.
A trailing 20-year tonnage-weighted benchmark can represent the physical structure of Ghana's export economy.
GHS denomination makes the benchmark locally relevant for Ghanaian institutional investors.
Ghana serves as a major corridor gateway for landlocked Burkina Faso and northern trade routes.

benchmark_methodology.weight_definition: Each constituent weight is the commodity's share of trailing twenty-year average export tonnage.
benchmark_methodology.transport_basis_note: Use outbound export tonnage from Takoradi, Tema, and other Ghanaian ports plus official trade statistics.
benchmark_methodology.calculation_logic: Index level changes according to weighted commodity return movements in GHS.
benchmark_methodology.coverage_rule: Include major raw materials exported from Ghana, even where no liquid futures market exists.
benchmark_methodology.methodology_caution: Pure tonnage weighting materially reduces the weight of high-value low-mass exports such as gold.

constituent_universe.name: Cocoa Beans
constituent_universe.export_note: Ghana is the world's second-largest cocoa producer. COCOBOD regulates the sector.

constituent_universe.name: Gold
constituent_universe.export_note: Gold is Ghana's largest export by value but minimal by tonnage. Major mines: Tarkwa, Obuasi, Ahafo.

constituent_universe.name: Crude Oil
constituent_universe.export_note: Jubilee and TEN fields offshore. Production began 2010. Exported via FPSO to global markets.

constituent_universe.name: Manganese Ore
constituent_universe.export_note: Ghana Manganese Company (Nsuta mine). Major global supplier. Shipped from Takoradi.

constituent_universe.name: Bauxite
constituent_universe.export_note: Awaso mine (Ghana Bauxite Company). Ghana has large untapped reserves including the Nyinahin deposit.

constituent_universe.name: Timber and Wood Products
constituent_universe.export_note: Tropical hardwoods from Western and Ashanti regions. Increasingly regulated for sustainability.

illustrative_weight_note.explanation: Illustrative weights come from available trade data estimates and should be replaced with official twenty-year shipment data from Ghana Statistical Service and Customs before institutional launch.

country_context.corridor_relevance: Ghana is the primary corridor outlet for landlocked Burkina Faso. The Tema-Ouagadougou highway and rail links are critical trade arteries.
country_context.special_note: Detailed prototype. Priority member due to scale, commodity depth, dual cocoa-mining profile, and corridor relevance to Sahel countries.

regulatory_positioning.securities_regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission Ghana (SEC Ghana)
regulatory_positioning.collective_investment_note: A future collective investment product would require SEC Ghana approval under the Unit Trusts and Mutual Funds framework.
regulatory_positioning.product_separation_note: GHAEX Fund should be constituted and distributed separately from any microfinance or banking balance sheet.
regulatory_positioning.tax_considerations: Capital gains tax regime and withholding tax on investment income apply under Ghana Revenue Authority rules.
